AQA.name: Paper 1
AQA.title: Explorations in Creative Reading and Writing

Eduqas.name: Paper 1
Eduqas.title: 20th Century Literature Reading and Creative Prose Writing

Eduqas.name: Paper 2
Eduqas.title: 19th and 21st Century Non-fiction Reading and Transactional/Persuasive Writing

assessmentObjectives.description: Identify and interpret explicit and implicit information and ideas; select and synthesise evidence from different texts
Explain, comment on and analyse how writers use language and structure to achieve effects and influence readers
Compare writers' ideas and perspectives, and how these are conveyed across texts
Evaluate texts critically and support this with appropriate textual references
Communicate clearly, effectively and imaginatively, selecting and adapting tone, style and register for different forms, purposes and audiences
Use a range of vocabulary and sentence structures for clarity, purpose and effect, with accurate spelling and punctuation

topics.name: Retrieval and Literal Comprehension
topics.description: Finding and extracting explicit information from texts

topics.name: Inference and Deduction
topics.description: Reading between the lines to understand implied meanings

topics.name: Language Analysis
topics.description: Analysing how writers use language to create effects

topics.name: Structure Analysis
topics.description: Analysing how writers organise and structure their texts

topics.name: Evaluation
topics.description: Critically evaluating texts and supporting opinions with evidence

topics.name: Comparison
topics.description: Comparing texts and identifying similarities and differences

topics.name: Synthesis
topics.description: Combining information from multiple sources

topics.name: Understanding Perspectives
topics.description: Identifying and understanding writers' viewpoints and perspectives

topics.name: Descriptive Writing
topics.description: Writing descriptively to create vivid images and atmosphere

topics.name: Narrative Writing
topics.description: Writing engaging stories with plot, character and setting

topics.name: Vocabulary and Word Choice
topics.description: Selecting and using ambitious vocabulary effectively

topics.name: Sentence Structures
topics.description: Using varied sentence structures for effect

topics.name: Punctuation
topics.description: Using punctuation accurately and for effect

topics.name: Persuasive Writing
topics.description: Writing to persuade and convince an audience

topics.name: Letters
topics.description: Writing formal and informal letters

topics.name: Speeches
topics.description: Writing engaging speeches for different audiences

topics.name: Articles
topics.description: Writing newspaper and magazine articles

topics.name: Reviews
topics.description: Writing reviews of films, books, products, etc.

topics.name: Audience and Purpose
topics.description: Adapting writing for different audiences and purposes

topics.name: Spelling Rules and Patterns
topics.description: Understanding and applying spelling rules

topics.name: Punctuation for Effect
topics.description: Using punctuation to create specific effects

topics.name: Grammar and Syntax
topics.description: Understanding and using grammatical structures correctly

topics.name: Paragraphing
topics.description: Organising writing into effective paragraphs

notes.spokenLanguage: Spoken Language is a separate non-exam assessment, graded Pass/Merit/Distinction. It does not count towards the GCSE grade.
notes.paperDifferences: All boards assess similar skills but structure papers differently. AQA and Edexcel split into creative/imaginative and non-fiction/transactional papers.
notes.textTypes: Students encounter 19th, 20th and 21st century texts including fiction, non-fiction, literary non-fiction, journalism, speeches and essays.
